title: 10 best herbal creams for flu

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with flu because of its immune-boosting properties, which aid in fighting off the viral infection. The cream contains active compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. These properties help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as sore throats, coughs, and body aches. By applying the cream topically, Echinacea purpurea may provide localized relief, supporting the body's natural defenses against the flu virus. Its benefits are often experienced within a short period of time.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Helps reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system to prevent overactive responses that can lead to flu complications.
Cichoric acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: Have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and prevent excessive immune response that can contribute to flu severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They contain compounds such as caftaric acid and chicoric acid, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: They contain alkylamides, which are thought to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a sunny spot with well-drained soil.
Dry the harvested echinacea flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried echinacea with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with flu because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound eucalyptol in these creams helps to open up airways, making it easier to breathe. This relief is especially beneficial for flu sufferers experiencing congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has antimicrobial properties that may help combat underlying infections, while its cooling sensation can provide temporary pain relief, making it a popular remedy to alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relieve congestion and cough associated with flu.
Catechin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing fever and alleviating body aches and pains associated with flu.
α-Pinene: Demonstrates decongestant properties, helping to clear nasal passages and relieve sinus pressure caused by flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a key compound responsible for the plant's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks contain eucalyptol and other compounds that help in reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, making them useful in creams for flu.

Essential oils extracted from Leaves: The essential oils extracted from the leaves contain compounds like eucalyptol, which are used in creams to help relieve flu symptoms such as cough, congestion, and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and aroma.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Infuse 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids.
Blend 10 milliliters of the infused oil with 90 milliliters of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with flu because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce congestion and alleviate symptoms such as fever and cough. The warming sensation from the cream also helps to increase blood circulation, which can aid in the body's natural defense against the flu virus. Additionally, ginger's immune-boosting properties may help to shorten the duration and severity of the flu. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a popular natural remedy for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which help combat the flu virus and prevent its spread in the body.

Volatile oils (including terpenes): The volatile oils in ginger, including terpenes, have expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and cough associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are often used as an alternative to rhizomes, as they share similar properties and contain compounds that help to alleviate flu symptoms.
Barks: Although less commonly used, ginger barks are sometimes used in traditional medicine to make creams, as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter and place them in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler at 180f for 10 minutes.
Add 50g of zingiber officinale essential oil and 50g of jojoba oil to the melted mixture.
Stir in 25g of vitamin e oil and 10g of preservative and let the mixture cool to 120f.
Pour the cooled mixture into containers and let them cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and glycosides, stimulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. By inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus, Echinacea angustifolia creams alleviate congestion, fever, and body aches, providing relief and comfort to those affected. Its soothing and calming properties also promote relaxation, aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the severity of flu symptoms by minimizing swelling and pain in the body.
Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms to fight off the flu virus.
Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides possess antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth and replication of the flu virus, thereby reducing the risk of infection and severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are used because of their rich content of flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: They are used due to their high concentration of phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 500ml of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours or overnight then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine 200g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Add 50ml of the echinacea liquid to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers and let cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with flu because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with flu. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying virus causing the flu. As a result, glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief and support during the recovery process, promoting a faster and more comfortable recovery.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory tract, which can alleviate flu symptoms such as congestion and cough.
Flavonoids: Exhibit antiviral properties that can help combat the influenza virus, reducing its replication and severity of the flu.
Glycyrrhizin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, body aches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve flu symptoms.
Roots: The roots also contain flavonoids, which exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that aid in soothing flu-related respiratory issues.
Roots: Additionally, the roots have been traditionally used for their mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from flu-related congestion.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven for 24 hours at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind 100 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powder with 100 grams of distilled water in a double boiler and heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 70 degrees celsius.
Combine 10 grams of the herbal extract with 50 grams of emulsifying wax and 20 grams of vegetable oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Blend the mixture for 10 minutes using an immersion blender to create a smooth and consistent cream texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with flu because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to combat viral and bacterial infections, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. By inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help alleviate congestion, cough, and fever associated with the flu. Additionally, its antiviral properties may help reduce the risk of complications and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an expectorant to help relieve congestion and cough associated with flu.
Limonene: Exhibits antiviral properties to inhibit the growth of flu viruses and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Methyleugenol: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties to help combat secondary bacterial infections that often accompany flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antiviral and antibacterial properties to help combat flu symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that can aid in reducing fever and relieving congestion associated with the flu.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used to make creams for flu relief, as they contain compounds that can help soothe coughs and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Distill 500ml of water using a steam distillation apparatus to extract essential oil from melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Combine 50g of distilled water with 25g of glycerin and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Add 20ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with flu because they contain thymol, a natural compound that has antiviral and antimicrobial properties. Thymol works by inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus, thereby reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Additionally, thymol's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from flu-related discomforts. This makes Thymus vulgaris creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating flu symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an antimicrobial agent to combat the influenza virus and other pathogens that cause flu symptoms.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate flu-related respiratory issues and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used due to their high content of thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which help combat flu symptoms.

Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used for their high concentration of essential oils, including thymol, which provide antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe flu-related congestion and discomfort.
Seeds: Thymus vulgaris seeds are sometimes used in herbal remedies for their expectorant properties, which help to relieve coughs and congestion associated with the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steep the fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil.
Strain the oil infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix the thymus vulgaris oil infusion with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler.
Pour the thymus vulgaris cream mixture into small jars and let it cool and set before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum creams helps with flu because they contain compounds that have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredients in these creams, such as allicin and sulfur compounds, work together to combat the underlying causes of flu, including viral and bacterial infections. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy immune response, Allium sativum creams can provide relief from flu symptoms, such as congestion, cough, and body aches, helping to alleviate discomfort and support the body's natural recovery process. This can aid in a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: It has antiviral properties that help combat the flu virus by inhibiting its replication and reducing the severity of the symptoms.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help alleviate the flu symptoms, such as congestion, cough, and fever, by reducing the production of inflammatory mediators.
S-Allylcysteine: It has immunomodulatory effects that help boost the immune system and reduce the duration and severity of the flu by stimulating the production of cytokines and other immune cells.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds like allicin, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate flu symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Allium sativum are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to create remedies for colds and flu due to their potential to boost immunity.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used to create a soothing cream that can help reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues associated with flu.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried allium sativum rhizomes and infuse them in 1l of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 14 days.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt them.
Combine the strained oil with the melted wax and butter mixture in a 3:2 ratio.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before transferring it to jars.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with flu because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are key factors in the development and severity of the flu. By applying Ginkgo biloba creams topically, these compounds can be absorbed into the skin, helping to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms such as congestion, cough, and body aches associated with the flu. This can aid in faster recovery and relief from flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate flu symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the nasal passages, which may help relieve congestion and sinus pressure.
Bilobalide: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Ginkgolides: These compounds are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help combat the underlying viral infection causing the flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate flu symptoms.

Buds: Buds of Ginkgo biloba are used in creams for flu because they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids compared to leaves, which can provide enhanced relief from flu symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in creams for flu, particularly for their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the flu.
remedy_recipe: Infuse ginkgo biloba leaves in a carrier oil such as jojoba oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Combine the infused ginkgo biloba oil with beeswax and shea butter in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add a natural preservative such as vitamin e oil at a dosage of 0.5% to the mixture.
Pour the cream into clean, sterilized containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with flu because they contain the active compound elderberry, which has antiviral properties that can help combat the influenza virus. The creams stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Additionally, elderberry has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate congestion, cough, and sore throats, providing relief from flu-related discomfort. This makes Sambucus nigra creams a popular natural remedy for flu prevention and treatment.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and have antiviral properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms and shorten the duration of the illness.
Anthocyanins: These pigments have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever and body aches.
Sambunigrin: This glycoside has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to inhibit the replication of the influenza virus and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are commonly used to make creams for flu due to their high content of antiviral compounds, particularly sambunigrin and sambucin.
Leaves: The leaves are used in flu creams due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as elderberries, are used to make creams for flu due to their high antioxidant content and ability to boost the immune system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of sambucus nigra berries in late summer when they turn dark purple and are ripe for use.
Extract the juice from the berries using a juicer or blender to obtain 1/2 cup of juice.
Mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of the sambucus nigra juice to the melted mixture and stir well to combine ingredients.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for flu are topical remedies infused with natural herbs that provide relief from flu symptoms, such as congestion and body aches, by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The best herbal creams for flu are Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea angustifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Allium sativum, Ginkgo biloba, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, flu can lead to severe respiratory complications, missed work or school, and a weakened immune system, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.
